task: Adopt this design system as the definitive visual direction for Lyzane, replacing prior exploratory directions (Atelier Noir marquee/cursor concepts, burgundy-forward palette) with what is shown in the reference mockups and official logo
supersedes_note: This design system takes priority over conflicting decisions made in earlier changesets: burgundy shifts from a primary accent to a minor functional-only color, hero/section layout moves from centered/stacked to photography-led asymmetric splits, and headline typography moves from tracked-uppercase to sentence-case serif. The 3D shoe hero, admin panel structure, and cart/checkout logic from earlier changesets remain valid and are not affected by this file.

logo.asset_file: public/images/logo1v1.png (existing) -- treat the graphic mark as a fixed asset, do not recreate the metallic ribbon/S swoosh in CSS

wordmark_typography.style: geometric sans-serif, architectural feel with slightly customized letterforms
recommended_web_fonts: Josefin Sans (already in project as --font-josefin / font-brand -- keep, but increase tracking from 0.25em to 0.35em to match reference more closely)
Montserrat Light/ExtraLight as an alternative if Josefin Sans doesn't feel geometric enough once tracked wider
wordmark_typography.notes: If exact character shapes matter (the reference logo's Y and A have subtly custom cuts), the wordmark in the navbar/footer should render as the logo image asset, not live text -- only use the font substitution for any place the brand name appears as plain text (e.g. metadata, loading states)

tagline.placement: directly beneath the wordmark in navbar/hero, and in the footer brand column

near_black.usage: Hero backgrounds, dark section backgrounds, footer, navbar -- replaces the flatter #1a1a1a charcoal from the prior spec with a warmer near-black matching the reference photography's shadow tones

warm_cream.usage: Light section backgrounds -- replaces the cooler #f5f5f5 cream from the prior spec with a warmer, slightly deeper cream matching the reference's paper-like tone

brass_accent.usage: Primary CTA buttons on light backgrounds (filled), link underlines, small accent details -- used sparingly, one accent moment per section, not as a dominant color

burgundy_functional_only.usage: DEMOTED from primary accent to functional-only: cart item count badge, destructive/error states, admin status pills only. Do not use for primary CTA buttons or decorative accents going forward

headline.case: sentence case, NOT uppercase -- e.g. 'Designed to last.' not 'DESIGNED TO LAST'
headline.notes: This is a departure from the earlier Atelier Noir direction which used ultra-light weight tracked uppercase -- references consistently show warm, readable sentence-case serif headlines

nav_and_labels.usage: Nav links, eyebrow labels (e.g. 'COLLECTION', 'JOURNAL'), product category labels, button text

body.color: muted_gray on light, muted_cream on dark

sequence: Navbar (transparent over hero, solidifies on scroll)
HeroCarousel (full-bleed photography, left-aligned text block, pagination dots, scroll hint)
SplitFeature (image + text, alternating side per instance)
FeatureStrip (4-column dark photography grid with numbered captions)
JournalSection (full-bleed editorial photography + dark text panel)
Footer (four-column: brand, shop links, help links, newsletter+social)

changes: Background: transparent over the hero image, transitioning to near_black (#0d0c0a) with a subtle bottom border once scrolled past hero height (pair with the existing hide-on-scroll behavior from prior changesets -- this replaces 'hide' with 'solidify')
Logo: use the actual logo image asset (mark + wordmark), sized to match reference proportions (mark roughly 28-32px tall, wordmark text sized to align optically with mark)
Nav links: uppercase, 12px, tracking 0.15em, color cream at 70% opacity, hover to full opacity cream, active link gets a thin underline (1px, cream or brass)
Right side icons: search, account/user, cart -- bare icon style (no button borders), consistent with existing near-invisible-chrome principle
Cart badge: keep burgundy per the demoted functional-only role

HeroCarousel.description: Replaces/extends the existing ShoeHero for the top-of-page moment -- can either wrap the existing 3D scene as one carousel slide, or sit as a photography-led alternative depending on whether real product photography exists yet
layout: Full-bleed background: 3D scene (existing) OR high-res product photography once shot, with a dark gradient overlay on the left third for text legibility (linear-gradient(90deg, rgba(13,12,10,0.75) 0%, rgba(13,12,10,0.2) 50%, transparent 80%))
Text block: left-aligned, vertically centered-low (around 45-55% viewport height), NOT centered on the page
Eyebrow: 'TIMELESS BY NATURE' per tagline spec above
Headline: sentence-case serif, e.g. 'Designed to last.', two lines max
CTA: text link style 'EXPLORE COLLECTION' with a small arrow icon, NOT a filled button -- thin bottom border, uppercase, tracked, matches reference's understated hero CTA
Pagination indicator: bottom-left, '01 — 02 — 03' style, small tracked numerals, indicates carousel position if multiple hero slides exist
Scroll hint: bottom-right, small vertical 'SCROLL' label with a thin descending line or chevron

SplitFeature.description: Replaces the centered NarrativeSection pattern with an image+text split matching the reference's second section
layout: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2, image column full-height object-cover, text column padded and vertically centered

Text column: small uppercase eyebrow optional, serif headline sentence-case, body paragraph in muted color, a text-link CTA styled as 'OUR PHILOSOPHY +' -- label plus a thin plus/arrow icon, not a button

FeatureStrip.description: Four-column dark photography grid with numbered micro-captions, matching reference image 1's 'CUIR ITALIEN / FINITIONS ARTISANALES / CONFORT NATUREL / STYLE INTEMPOREL' section
layout: grid grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-4, each cell: full-bleed close-up product photography background, dark gradient overlay at bottom for text legibility
Per cell: small numeral (01-04) top-left, short label (uppercase, tracked) and one-line description at bottom, cream text

JournalSection.description: Editorial full-bleed section matching reference's 'JOURNAL -- Stories of craft and character' pattern
layout: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-[1fr_1.3fr], left column near_black background with eyebrow 'JOURNAL', serif headline, text-link CTA 'DISCOVER JOURNAL' with arrow
right column full-bleed editorial lifestyle photography, object-cover

ProductGrid.description: Add a landing-page 'collection preview' variant of the existing product grid matching reference's 4-card layout with 'VIEW ALL' link
layout: Section eyebrow 'COLLECTION', serif headline e.g. 'Essential forms. Endless occasions.', 'VIEW ALL' text-link top-right of the section header row

4-column grid on desktop, each card: product photography on warm_cream background (not pure white), product name uppercase small tracked, price, small '+' quick-add icon button bottom-right of image on hover

changes: Background: warm_cream (light footer, matching reference) rather than dark -- note this differs from a fully dark footer if previously implemented; reference footer 2 and 3 use light footer, reference 1 uses dark footer, so a light footer with a dark logo lockup is the more common pattern across the three and is recommended default
Four columns: Brand (logo + tagline + one-line description), Shop links, Help links, Stay Connected (email input + subscribe + social icons)
Social icons: Instagram, Facebook, TikTok -- bare icon style matching reference
Bottom bar: copyright + Privacy Policy + Terms links, small tracked uppercase, centered or left-aligned per existing footer structure

verification_steps: Confirm the wordmark renders as the actual logo image asset in the navbar and footer, not as live CSS text, to guarantee exact letterform match
Confirm live-text instances of 'LYZANE' (metadata, loading states, favicon alt text) use the adjusted 0.35em tracking on Josefin Sans as the closest approximation
Confirm burgundy no longer appears as a primary CTA color anywhere on the landing page -- only on the cart badge and admin status pills
Confirm all section headlines are sentence-case serif, not tracked uppercase
Confirm the near_black (#0d0c0a) and warm_cream (#f2ede6) values replace the previous #1a1a1a / #f5f5f5 tokens consistently across landing components
Confirm CTA links in SplitFeature and JournalSection render as understated text-links with icons, not filled buttons -- reserve filled brass buttons for primary hero/product-grid actions only
Confirm the ProductGrid quick-add '+' button only appears on hover/focus, keeping the resting card state calm and photography-led
Cross-check that these visual changes do not regress the WhatsApp checkout flow, cart drawer logic, or admin panel from prior changesets -- this file is visual/typographic only
